competency_groups.title: Anatomy of Flowering Plants Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the anatomy and functions of tissue systems in dicots.
competencies: Identify and describe the three main tissue systems in flowering plants.
Differentiate between simple and complex permanent tissues based on structure and function.
Analyze the structural adaptations of plant tissues for support, transport, and protection.
competency_groups.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

competency_groups.title: Structural Organisation in Animals Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring the morphology, anatomy, and functions of animal digestive systems.
competencies: Map the anatomical structure of the human alimentary canal and associated glands.
Explain the physiological mechanisms involved in digestion, absorption, and assimilation.
Correlate structural features of organs with their specific functional roles.
competency_groups.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

competency_groups.title: Cell-The Unit of Life Competencies
competency_groups.description: Grasping the cell theory and the fundamental role of cells as life units.
competencies: State the postulates of the Cell Theory and its historical development.
Compare prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell structures to identify key differences.
Justify why the cell is considered the basic structural and functional unit of life.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Competencies
competency_groups.description: Analyzing the chemical constituents that form living cells.
competencies: Classify biomolecules into carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids based on structure.
Describe the monomeric units and polymerization processes of major biological macromolecules.
Evaluate the functional significance of enzymes as biological catalysts in cellular metabolism.

competency_groups.title: Cell Cycle and Cell Division Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the processes of mitosis, meiosis, and their biological significance.
competencies: Outline the phases of the cell cycle and distinguish between interphase and M-phase events.
Differentiate between mitotic and meiotic division in terms of chromosome behavior and outcome.
Explain the importance of cell division for growth, repair, reproduction, and genetic variation.
